speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Was ever king that joyed an earthly throne

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And could command no more content than I?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: No sooner was I crept out of my cradle

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But I was made a king at nine months old.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Was never subject longed to be a king

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: As I do long and wish to be a subject!

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Health and glad tidings to your Majesty!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, Buckingham, is the traitor Cade surprised,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Or is he but retired to make him strong?

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: He is fled, my lord, and all his powers do yield

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: And, humbly thus, with halters on their necks,

speaker: CLIFFORD
text: Expect your Highness’ doom of life or death.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Then, heaven, set ope thy everlasting gates

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To entertain my vows of thanks and praise!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Soldiers, this day have you redeemed your lives

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And showed how well you love your prince and country.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Continue still in this so good a mind,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And Henry, though he be infortunate,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Assure yourselves, will never be unkind.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And so with thanks and pardon to you all,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I do dismiss you to your several countries.

speaker: ALL
text: God save the King! God save the King!

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Please it your Grace to be advertisèd

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The Duke of York is newly come from Ireland

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And, with a puissant and a mighty power

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Of gallowglasses and stout kerns,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Is marching hitherward in proud array,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And still proclaimeth, as he comes along,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: His arms are only to remove from thee

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The Duke of Somerset, whom he terms a traitor.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thus stands my state, ’twixt Cade and York distressed,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Like to a ship that, having scaped a tempest,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Is straightway calmed and boarded with a pirate.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But now is Cade driven back, his men dispersed,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And now is York in arms to second him.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I pray thee, Buckingham, go and meet him,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And ask him what’s the reason of these arms.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Tell him I’ll send Duke Edmund to the Tower.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And, Somerset, we will commit thee thither

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Until his army be dismissed from him.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: My lord,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: I’ll yield myself to prison willingly,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Or unto death, to do my country good.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: In any case, be not too rough in terms,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For he is fierce and cannot brook hard language.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I will, my lord, and doubt not so to deal

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As all things shall redound unto your good.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Come, wife, let’s in, and learn to govern better,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For yet may England curse my wretched reign.
